behavior_summary: Provides Corinth NPC behaviour: a 'U8P_CORINTH::look' handler that barks a short greeting depending on whether the NPC has been met before, theft response logic that warns or calls guards, schedule management that records/updates the NPC's schedule state, and area-triggered behaviours that spawn short looping activity routines when the player enters or leaves a fast-area trigger.

The NPC uses global flags (corinthMet, corinthSchedule, stolenOnce, guardsCalled, somebodyTalking) to select greetings, escalate responses to theft, and choose which activity routine to run when the player approaches.

Runtime actions are implemented by spawning engine methods (animations/movements), using Item::bark for short dialog, pathfinding to targets during roaming behaviours, and interacting with the FREE subsystem for delays and queries.
notable_control_flow: CORINTH::look (main entry) reads global corinthMet and barks either a first-meeting string or a repeat-meeting string, then suspends.

U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea checks NPC liveliness and corinthSchedule, resets area/item references, and spawns one of several behavior processes (e.g., U8P_CORINTH::0364 or U8P_CORINTH::09B5) depending on schedule value.

U8P_CORINTH::AvatarStoleSomething classifies the stolen item by type; on first observed theft it sets stolenOnce and issues a warning bark, on subsequent/serious thefts it shouts, delays via FREE::1C19, and spawns CALGUARD::0080 to summon guards (guardsCalled prevents duplicate calls).

U8P_CORINTH::0364 is a looping behavior that inspects frames/shapes, may pathfind to chosen targets, avoids interrupting when somebodyTalking is true, and issues barks/animations via spawned METHOD::* helpers.
open_questions: Exact meanings and IDs of item types that trigger escalation in AvatarStoleSomething (type id comparisons are observed but specific named types are inferred).
Precise behaviors implemented by METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, METHOD::133F, METHOD::143A and how they differ (animations, small moves, or state changes are inferred).
Behavior and purpose of U8P_CORINTH::09B5 (referenced by schedule/enterFastArea) are not present in the excerpt; its role is inferred to be an alternate activity routine.
Exact semantics and index meanings for FREE::28F9 and FREE::1CE3 (they are queried and their numeric results are used to choose branches, but concrete mapping to world state is only inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_CORINTH::look
main_function.purpose: Handle player 'U8P_CORINTH::look' (examine) event for the Corinth NPC: set up item/process info state, choose and play a short greeting/bark based on whether the NPC has been met before (corinthMet), and then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0: invoked when the player examines or 'looks at' the Corinth NPC (player process id passed as caller).

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::look
function_map.role: Respond to player 'U8P_CORINTH::look' event; produce greeting and set process info/exclude flags.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React to a theft by the avatar: clear references, classify stolen item, warn or escalate to calling guards, set stolenOnce on first theft.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::schedule
function_map.role: Compute and store a schedule byte into corinthSchedule, avoid changes if NPC is dead, spawn methods to apply schedule transitions.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: when an actor (avatar) enters, clear references and spawn the appropriate activity routine based on corinthSchedule.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: clean/clear the area-related reference via FREE::2A56.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::0364
function_map.role: Looping activity routine: inspect frames/nearby items, attempt pathfinding to targets, manage barks/animations and timing; avoids interrupting existing speech.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::04DC
function_map.role: Proximity/conversation routine: choose among multiple short dialogue/animation branches using randomness and small delays; used to produce varied greetings or comments.

function_map.name: U8P_CORINTH::09B5
function_map.role: Alternate activity routine spawned for some schedule states (inferred from enterFastArea spawning it when corinthSchedule==2).

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the main roaming/behavior loop when corinthSchedule indicates schedule==1.
Spawn an alternate activity routine when corinthSchedule indicates schedule==2.

external_calls.purpose: Display a short greeting string to the player (first-meeting vs repeat greeting).
Clear stored item/marker references associated with the NPC when theft is observed.
Classify the stolen item to determine whether to escalate and call guards.
Query NPC facing to play a direction-specific short animation (METHOD::15E7) when reacting to theft.
Spawn/notify the guard AI to respond to a serious or repeated theft.
Query free-system state to decide which schedule branch to take when computing corinthSchedule.
Attempt movement toward chosen target coordinates during looping behavior; success/failure selects next actions.
Inspect frames/shapes of nearby items/tiles when picking pathing or interaction targets.
Set short delays between barks/animations in the looping behavior.
Clear/clean an area-related reference (cleanup on fast-area exit).

reads.name: corinthMet
reads.likely_effect: Selects which greeting string to bark in CORINTH::look (0 => first-meeting greeting, nonzero => repeat greeting).

reads.name: guardsCalled
reads.likely_effect: Used as a guard to prevent re-calling guards multiple times from AvatarStoleSomething.

reads.name: corinthSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Determines which behavior routine to spawn on area entry (enterFastArea) and is read by schedule()/enterFastArea to branch behavior.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Gating flag consulted by looping behavior (0364) to avoid interrupting existing dialog/speech.

writes.name: stolenOnce
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 on the first observed theft to change subsequent responses (first theft gives mild warning, later thefts escalate).

writes.name: corinthSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Updated by schedule() to persist the computed schedule/state for the NPC, used later by enterFastArea and behavior selection.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides Corinth NPC behaviour: a 'U8P_CORINTH::look' handler that barks a short greeting depending on whether the NPC has been met before, theft response logic that warns or calls guards, schedule management that records/updates the NPC's schedule state, and area-triggered behaviours that spawn short looping activity routines when the player enters or leaves a fast-area trigger.
